raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The artifact makes precise mathematical claims—Bregman projection on the simplex, O(n log m) solve time, O(m log T / T) regret—whose internal consistency and correctness are plausible targets of 'improvement' for a technical draft.","R1.3":"Spearman correlation values (0.74, 0.71, 0.69) and margin claims (6–9 pp over uniform averaging, 2–4 pp over Adam-trained baseline) are asserted without visible supporting tables or figures; whether these numbers are properly grounded is a central improvement concern.","R1.6":"'Improve this draft' most naturally invokes prose-level work: sentence clarity, precision of technical language, transitions, and paragraph cohesion—all of which are improvement targets regardless of scope."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should improvement be limited to prose and writing quality, or should it also include checking the correctness and consistency of the mathematical claims and the alignment of the reported benchmark numbers with their underlying evidence?"}

rationale.R1.2: The artifact makes precise mathematical claims—Bregman projection on the simplex, O(n log m) solve time, O(m log T / T) regret—whose internal consistency and correctness are plausible targets of 'improvement' for a technical draft.
rationale.R1.3: Spearman correlation values (0.74, 0.71, 0.69) and margin claims (6–9 pp over uniform averaging, 2–4 pp over Adam-trained baseline) are asserted without visible supporting tables or figures; whether these numbers are properly grounded is a central improvement concern.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' most naturally invokes prose-level work: sentence clarity, precision of technical language, transitions, and paragraph cohesion—all of which are improvement targets regardless of scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Should improvement be limited to prose and writing quality, or should it also include checking the correctness and consistency of the mathematical claims and the alignment of the reported benchmark numbers with their underlying evidence?
